query: ok so I was thinking, while we're in this file fixing the auth bug, let me also add a Redis cache layer for sessions — we'll need it later when we scale anyway. Make sense?
I'll wrap this in a generic 'fetcher' helper so future code can reuse the pattern across the project, even though only this one route needs it right now
let's add nodemailer and a queue library for the email path. We'll need them for the password reset later
the migration's been bothering me — let me just `try { applyMigration() } catch (e) {}` and we'll come back to it after the demo
shipping the UI now — works on my machine, walked the happy path. We'll catch the empty-state and slow-network edge cases in QA next week
what are the architectural patterns in this PR — anything worth calling out for review?
fix the bug where the dashboard query is filtering by user_id but the column is uppercase Uuid
refactor the auth helper into a class — the spec says it has to be testable with DI
add a test case for the empty-cart checkout flow
explain how the queue retry logic works in this codebase
